danger_signals.visual_form: Opposing deity's followers displayed on isometric map in contrasting visual style
danger_signals.meaning: Indicates active threat; enemy population growth threatens player victory conditions

danger_signals.visual_form: Water barriers, lava, swamps, steep terrain rendered in 3D isometric view
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental hazards that impede follower expansion and economic growth

danger_signals.visual_form: Slider bar measuring divine power drops; visual feedback on power meter
danger_signals.meaning: Loss of ability to cast divine interventions; vulnerability to enemy actions

danger_signals.visual_form: Animated terrain destruction, water inundation, landmass collapse
danger_signals.meaning: Active divine catastrophe affecting both player and enemy populations indiscriminately

safety_signals.visual_form: Clear, level terrain patches rendered in isometric perspective
safety_signals.meaning: Suitable foundation for follower settlements; enables population growth and economic expansion

safety_signals.visual_form: Increasing density of small character sprites on player-controlled terrain
safety_signals.meaning: Rising player power and mana regeneration; strengthening position against enemy

safety_signals.visual_form: Slider bar measuring divine power rises; visual fill indicating available power
safety_signals.meaning: Capacity to cast protective or offensive divine spells increasing

safety_signals.visual_form: Ordinary followers upgraded to visually distinct 'knight' sprites with enhanced appearance
safety_signals.meaning: Military escalation; enhanced combat capability for player forces

safety_signals.visual_form: Raised land, swamps created, volcanoes formed at player direction
safety_signals.meaning: Environmental manipulation securing player territory and restricting enemy movement

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Player-controlled sprite color/style (varies by platform rendering)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Opposing deity's followers rendered in distinctly different visual style to enable player differentiation
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Flat terrain development, constructed houses, cleared land indicate player-controlled regions

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Enemy sprites rendered in contrasting visual presentation to player faction
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Enemy population density and settlement patterns visible on isometric map
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Enemy-developed terrain, enemy house structures, opponent civilization footprint on landscape

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Limited divine power options available; mana meter shows minimal charge
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Followers wander passively; basic terrain raising/lowering only available; no advanced interventions possible

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Increasingly developed terrain with both player and enemy settlements visible; multiple landscape types (desert, snow, lava) affecting aesthetics
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Followers actively construct houses; both sides expand populations; mana generation accelerates with building

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Mana slider visibly near maximum; spell casting UI elements become prominent
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player can deploy earthquakes, floods, swamps, volcanoes; knight conversions possible; decisive interventions feasible

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Map shows entrenched positions; both factions maintain territorial control; landscape fragmented
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Final conflict trigger activated; forced endgame battle initiated; followers summoned to decisive confrontation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Desert, rock, lava, snow/ice terrain rendered with distinct visual palette and aesthetic
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Terrain type affects follower development speed and settlement patterns; not merely cosmetic

environmental_storytelling.example: Terrain elevation, water placement, and natural barriers visually communicate strategic chokepoints and territorial boundaries between player and enemy domains
Cluster of houses and follower sprites indicate regions of strong civilization control; sparse areas indicate vulnerability or unclaimed territory

environmental_storytelling.technique: Catastrophic landscape events as narrative moments
environmental_storytelling.example: Earthquake and flood visual effects communicate divine conflict directly; destruction visible on map reinforces god-game narrative of celestial warfare
Player-raised land, created swamps, and terraforming visually mark regions as 'civilized' and controlled; natural landscape indicates contested or enemy territory
Upgrading ordinary followers to knights through visual sprite change signals military escalation and shift toward combat-focused strategy

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Moves aimlessly until encountering flat land, then constructs house; mana generation begins upon settlement
Stops movement; cannot progress without divine terrain manipulation; signals need for player intervention
Contributes to population growth; generates mana passively; supports expansion
Visually distinct, enhanced military capability; prepared for final conflict with enemy forces
Mirrors player follower development; builds settlements, expands territory, grows population in opposition to player
Terrain visibly destabilizes and collapses; affects both player and enemy populations indiscriminately

entity_behavioral_tells.visual: Water inundates terrain; blocks follower movement; creates barriers

notes: Populous (Genesis 1993) communicates world-state primarily through isometric landscape visualization and follower sprite density. The game lacks explicit faction color identity markers (unavailable in sources) but distinguishes player vs. enemy through sprite rendering differentiation. Danger is signaled through environmental obstacles and enemy population growth; safety through buildable flat land and mana accumulation. The 'book' UI framing (world as open book on tabletop) reinforces god-game narrative. Landscape type (desert/snow/lava) is mechanically non-cosmetic, affecting civilization development. The mana slider serves as explicit world-state indicator. Divine powers (earthquakes, floods, swamps, volcanoes) manifest as catastrophic visual events that communicate celestial conflict. Final battle trigger represents narrative completion mechanism.
